mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate over a 3-month period signals a deteriorating labor market, which reduces aggregate consumer demand and forward-looking corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% is elevated from a historical perspective, suggesting labor market slack.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in UNRATE and 60-day forward returns is greater than -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near the zero lower bound, an acceleration in CPI inflation increases the probability of a future monetary tightening cycle, raising the discount rate on future earnings and negatively impacting equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is currently 0.09, satisfying the ZIRP trigger condition.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the correlation between the 3-month change in CPIAUCSL and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales is a direct indicator of robust consumer demand, which positively drives corporate revenue expectations and near-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The 12-month rolling correlation between the 1-month percent change in RSAFS and 20-day forward returns is consistently below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year contraction in industrial production indicates a significant slowdown in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy, signaling lower future GDP growth and corporate earnings, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime, strong month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a labor market recovery without immediate inflation risk, boosting expectations for future economic growth and corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE of 6.7% meets the trigger condition for a high unemployment regime.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6.0%, the correlation between monthly PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts indicates waning consumer confidence and investment in durable goods, which negatively impacts forward-looking growth expectations and equity prices, especially when overall economic growth is already weak.
failure_condition: The negative relationship between HOUST changes and forward returns disappears or reverses during periods of above-trend GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate provides a powerful tailwind for equities by lowering the risk-free rate used for discounting future cash flows and encouraging risk-taking behavior among investors. This effect is most pronounced over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS of 0.09% is well within the low-rate trigger condition.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is below 1% is not statistically higher than when it is above 1% over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of accelerating inflation and decelerating industrial production signals a stagflationary environment. This combination compresses corporate profit margins and increases economic uncertainty, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-mo change in CPIAUCSL > 0.5 AND 3-mo change in INDPRO < 0
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of cases.
